report_name: New security tools target deepfakes, risky OAuth, and AI agent threats

indicators.name: Suspicious OAuth Application Consent
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious OAuth Application Consent
id: 2b9a8f7c-1e3d-4b5a-9c8d-6f7a8b9c0d1e
status: experimental
description: 'Detects when a user grants consent to an OAuth application. This is a generic detection for a risky activity that could be associated with malicious applications gaining access to cloud services like Office 365 or Google Workspace.'
references:
  - https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/07/17/new-infosec-products-of-the-week-july-17-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-17
tags:
  - attack.t1528
level: medium
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.azuread
detection:
  selection:
    Operation: 'Consent to application'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects user consent to OAuth applications, a behavior that can be abused by malicious apps to gain access to data in cloud services as highlighted by Nudge Security's new capabilities.

indicators.name: Suspicious Browser Extension Installation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Browser Extension Installation
id: 8c1d2e3f-4b5a-4c8d-9a8b-7f6a5b4c3d2e
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the creation of files in common browser extension directories. While often legitimate, this activity can also indicate the installation of a malicious or high-risk browser extension, which is a threat vector addressed by new security tooling.'
references:
  - https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/07/17/new-infosec-products-of-the-week-july-17-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-17
tags:
  - attack.t1176.001
level: low
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Extensions\'
      - '\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\Default\Extensions\'
      - '\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects file creation events in browser extension paths, which could indicate the installation of a risky extension, a threat highlighted by Nudge Security.
